suppliers.context: Pursuant to the terms of a manufacturing agreement between both parties, VIS was obligated to use its best commercial efforts to manufacture wafers at specified yield rates for us up to a fixed amount of reserved capacity per month.

suppliers.entity: Systems on Silicon Manufacturing Company Pte. Ltd.
suppliers.context: SSMC’s business is limited to manufacturing wafers for us, our subsidiaries, NXP and NXP’s subsidiaries. While we, together with NXP, have the right to purchase up to 100% of SSMC’s annual capacity, we and NXP are required to purchase, in the aggregate, at least 70% of SSMC’s full capacity.
In January 2007, we acquired a 51.2% equity interest in Xintec, a supplier of wafer level packaging service, to support our CMOS image sensor manufacturing business.

single_source_dependencies.context: Also, since we procure some of our raw materials from sole-sourced suppliers, there is a risk that our need for such raw materials may not be met or that back-up supplies may not be readily available.

geographic_concentration.context: The majority of our principal executive officers and our principal production facilities are located in the R.O.C., and the majority of our net revenue is derived from our operations in the R.O.C.
TSMC Arizona owns a parcel of land of approximately 4,775,885 square meters in the State of Arizona where Fab 21 and related offices are located.
JASM owns a parcel of land of approximately 476,290 square meters in Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan, where Fab 23 and related offices are located.
ESMC owns a parcel of land of approximately 513,557 square meters in the City of Dresden in Germany, where Fab 24 and related facilities will be located.
TSMC Nanjing Company Limited (“TSMC Nanjing”) owns the land use rights of 453,401 square meters of land in Nanjing, where Fab 16 and related offices are located.

capacity_constraints.constraint: limited supply and/or long delivery cycles for equipment
capacity_constraints.context: Our operations and ongoing expansion plans depend on our ability to obtain necessary equipment and related services available from a limited number of suppliers. As a result, we may encounter the situation of limited supply and/or long delivery cycles.

Our operations may be interrupted, and our expansion may be limited, by power or other utility outages or shortages, and our financial results may be adversely affected by increased prices of power or other utilities.

supply_chain_risks.context: Also, any increase in the use of export control restrictions and sanctions to target certain countries and entities... could impact not only our ability to continue supplying products to those customers, but also our customers’ demand for our products, and could even lead to changes in semiconductor supply chains.

supply_chain_risks.risk: raw material shortages and price increases
supply_chain_risks.context: Our revenue and profitability may decline if we are unable to obtain adequate supplies of raw materials in a timely manner and at commercially reasonable prices. In the past, shortages in the supply of some materials... have resulted in occasional industry-wide price adjustments and delivery delays.
In April 2024 and January 2025, several earthquakes struck Taiwan, causing damage to our inventories, plant facilities, machinery and equipment.

revenue_concentration.context: Customer A accounted for 22% of net revenue in 2024.
Customer B accounted for 12% of net revenue in 2024.

While we generate revenue from hundreds of customers worldwide, our ten largest customers in 2022, 2023 and 2024 accounted for approximately, 68%, 70% and 76% of our net revenue in the respective year.

geographic_revenue.context: North America accounted for 70% of net revenue in 2024.
China accounted for 11% of net revenue in 2024.
Asia Pacific accounted for 10% of net revenue in 2024.
Japan accounted for 5% of net revenue in 2024.
EMEA accounted for 4% of net revenue in 2024.

purchase_obligations.obligation_type: Capital Purchase or Other Purchase Obligations
purchase_obligations.context: Represents commitments for construction or purchase of equipment, raw material and other property or services. These commitments were not recorded on our statement of financial position as of December 31, 2024.

market_risk_disclosures.exposure: NT dollar against U.S. dollar, Euro, and Japanese yen
market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: every 1% depreciation of the U.S. dollar against the NT dollar would result in an approximately 0.4 percentage point decrease in our operating margin
market_risk_disclosures.hedging: The Company uses foreign currency derivatives contracts, such as currency forwards or currency swaps, and non-derivative financial instruments, such as foreign currency denominated debts.
market_risk_disclosures.context: Substantially all of our sales are denominated in U.S. dollars and over half of our capital expenditures are denominated in currencies other than the NT dollar, primarily in U.S. dollars, Euros and Japanese yen.

market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: hypothetical adverse interest rate change of 100 basis points across all maturities would have decreased our other comprehensive income by NT$4,501 million in 2024
market_risk_disclosures.hedging: The Company has entered and may in the future enter into interest rate derivatives to partially hedge interest rate risk on its fixed income investments and anticipated debt issuance.
market_risk_disclosures.context: We are exposed to interest rate risks primarily in relation to our investment portfolio and outstanding debt.

inventory_composition.context: Work in process inventory as of December 31, 2024.
Raw materials inventory as of December 31, 2024.
Finished goods inventory as of December 31, 2024.
